defmodule SymphonyElixir.Codex.CommandPolicy do
  @moduledoc """
  Enforces integration boundaries on commands requested by BOS AgentRuns.

  This policy is deliberately evaluated on the complete shell command before
  Codex receives permission to launch it. Host-side workspace hooks and other
  Symphony administration do not pass through this module.
  """

  @type denial :: %{
          reason: String.t(),
          suggested_operation: String.t(),
          policy: String.t()
        }

  @gh_token ~r/(?:^|\s)(?:[^\s;|&()]*\/)?gh(?:\s|$|[;|&()])/i
  @github_api ~r/(?:https?:\/\/)?api\.github\.com(?:[\/:?#]|$)/i

  @doc """
  Returns a denial for direct GitHub integration commands and `:allow` for
  commands that remain inside the supported BOS boundary.
  """
  @spec evaluate(term()) :: :allow | {:deny, denial()}
  def evaluate(command) when is_list(command), do: command |> Enum.join(" ") |> evaluate()

  def evaluate(command) when is_binary(command) do
    normalized = normalize(command)

    cond do
      Regex.match?(@gh_token, normalized) ->
        {:deny,
         %{
           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
           reason: "Direct GitHub CLI execution is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
           suggested_operation: gh_replacement(normalized)
         }}

      Regex.match?(@github_api, normalized) ->
        {:deny,
         %{
           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
           reason: "Direct GitHub REST or GraphQL access is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
           suggested_operation: api_replacement(normalized)
         }}

      true ->
        :allow
    end
  end

  def evaluate(_command), do: :allow

  defp normalize(command) do
    command
    |> String.replace(~r/["'\\]/, "")
    |> String.replace(~r/\s+/, " ")
    |> String.trim()
    |> String.downcase()
  end

  defp gh_replacement(command) do
    cond do
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:create|edit)/, command) -> "bos_create_pull_request"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:merge|close)/, command) -> "bos_request_merge"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:checks|status)/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request_checks"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\b/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+issue\b/, command) -> "bos_get_issue"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+release\b/, command) -> "bos_create_release"
      true -> "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
    end
  end

  defp api_replacement(command) do
    cond do
      String.contains?(command, "/pulls") or String.contains?(command, "/graphql") ->
        "bos_get_pull_request or the corresponding bos-mcp pull-request operation"

      String.contains?(command, "/issues") ->
        "bos_get_issue or the corresponding bos-mcp issue operation"

      String.contains?(command, "/deployments") ->
        "bos_create_deployment or the corresponding bos-mcp deployment operation"

      String.contains?(command, "/releases") ->
        "bos_create_release"

      true ->
        "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
    end
  end
end

defmodule SymphonyElixir.Codex.CommandPolicy do
  @moduledoc """
  Enforces integration boundaries on commands requested by BOS AgentRuns.

  This policy is deliberately evaluated on the complete shell command before
  Codex receives permission to launch it. Host-side workspace hooks and other
  Symphony administration do not pass through this module.
  """

  @type denial :: %{
          reason: String.t(),
          suggested_operation: String.t(),
          policy: String.t()
        }

  @gh_token ~r/(?:^|\s)(?:[^\s;|&()]*\/)?gh(?:\s|$|[;|&()])/i
  @github_api ~r/(?:https?:\/\/)?api\.github\.com(?:[\/:?#]|$)/i

  @doc """
  Returns a denial for direct GitHub integration commands and `:allow` for
  commands that remain inside the supported BOS boundary.
  """
  @spec evaluate(term()) :: :allow | {:deny, denial()}
  def evaluate(command) when is_list(command), do: command |> Enum.join(" ") |> evaluate()

  def evaluate(command) when is_binary(command) do
    normalized = normalize(command)

    cond do
      Regex.match?(@gh_token, normalized) ->
        {:deny,
         %{
           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
           reason: "Direct GitHub CLI execution is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
           suggested_operation: gh_replacement(normalized)
         }}

      Regex.match?(@github_api, normalized) ->
        {:deny,
         %{
           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
           reason: "Direct GitHub REST or GraphQL access is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
           suggested_operation: api_replacement(normalized)
         }}

      true ->
        :allow
    end
  end

  def evaluate(_command), do: :allow

  defp normalize(command) do
    command
    |> String.replace(~r/["'\\]/, "")
    |> String.replace(~r/\s+/, " ")
    |> String.trim()
    |> String.downcase()
  end

  defp gh_replacement(command) do
    cond do
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:create|edit)/, command) -> "bos_create_pull_request"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:merge|close)/, command) -> "bos_request_merge"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:checks|status)/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request_checks"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\b/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+issue\b/, command) -> "bos_get_issue"
      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+release\b/, command) -> "bos_create_release"
      true -> "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
    end
  end

  defp api_replacement(command) do
    cond do
      String.contains?(command, "/pulls") or String.contains?(command, "/graphql") ->
        "bos_get_pull_request or the corresponding bos-mcp pull-request operation"

      String.contains?(command, "/issues") ->
        "bos_get_issue or the corresponding bos-mcp issue operation"

      String.contains?(command, "/deployments") ->
        "bos_create_deployment or the corresponding bos-mcp deployment operation"

      String.contains?(command, "/releases") ->
        "bos_create_release"

      true ->
        "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
    end
  end
end

diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..bb00efddd22b8ae7a56038c4d1b9e02893e379f8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+defmodule SymphonyElixir.Codex.CommandPolicy do
+  @moduledoc """
+  Enforces integration boundaries on commands requested by BOS AgentRuns.
+
+  This policy is deliberately evaluated on the complete shell command before
+  Codex receives permission to launch it. Host-side workspace hooks and other
+  Symphony administration do not pass through this module.
+  """
+
+  @type denial :: %{
+          reason: String.t(),
+          suggested_operation: String.t(),
+          policy: String.t()
+        }
+
+  @gh_token ~r/(?:^|\s)(?:[^\s;|&()]*\/)?gh(?:\s|$|[;|&()])/i
+  @github_api ~r/(?:https?:\/\/)?api\.github\.com(?:[\/:?#]|$)/i
+
+  @doc """
+  Returns a denial for direct GitHub integration commands and `:allow` for
+  commands that remain inside the supported BOS boundary.
+  """
+  @spec evaluate(term()) :: :allow | {:deny, denial()}
+  def evaluate(command) when is_list(command), do: command |> Enum.join(" ") |> evaluate()
+
+  def evaluate(command) when is_binary(command) do
+    normalized = normalize(command)
+
+    cond do
+      Regex.match?(@gh_token, normalized) ->
+        {:deny,
+         %{
+           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
+           reason: "Direct GitHub CLI execution is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
+           suggested_operation: gh_replacement(normalized)
+         }}
+
+      Regex.match?(@github_api, normalized) ->
+        {:deny,
+         %{
+           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
+           reason: "Direct GitHub REST or GraphQL access is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
+           suggested_operation: api_replacement(normalized)
+         }}
+
+      true ->
+        :allow
+    end
+  end
+
+  def evaluate(_command), do: :allow
+
+  defp normalize(command) do
+    command
+    |> String.replace(~r/["'\\]/, "")
+    |> String.replace(~r/\s+/, " ")
+    |> String.trim()
+    |> String.downcase()
+  end
+
+  defp gh_replacement(command) do
+    cond do
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:create|edit)/, command) -> "bos_create_pull_request"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:merge|close)/, command) -> "bos_request_merge"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:checks|status)/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request_checks"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\b/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+issue\b/, command) -> "bos_get_issue"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+release\b/, command) -> "bos_create_release"
+      true -> "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp api_replacement(command) do
+    cond do
+      String.contains?(command, "/pulls") or String.contains?(command, "/graphql") ->
+        "bos_get_pull_request or the corresponding bos-mcp pull-request operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/issues") ->
+        "bos_get_issue or the corresponding bos-mcp issue operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/deployments") ->
+        "bos_create_deployment or the corresponding bos-mcp deployment operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/releases") ->
+        "bos_create_release"
+
+

index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..bb00efddd22b8ae7a56038c4d1b9e02893e379f8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+defmodule SymphonyElixir.Codex.CommandPolicy do
+  @moduledoc """
+  Enforces integration boundaries on commands requested by BOS AgentRuns.
+
+  This policy is deliberately evaluated on the complete shell command before
+  Codex receives permission to launch it. Host-side workspace hooks and other
+  Symphony administration do not pass through this module.
+  """
+
+  @type denial :: %{
+          reason: String.t(),
+          suggested_operation: String.t(),
+          policy: String.t()
+        }
+
+  @gh_token ~r/(?:^|\s)(?:[^\s;|&()]*\/)?gh(?:\s|$|[;|&()])/i
+  @github_api ~r/(?:https?:\/\/)?api\.github\.com(?:[\/:?#]|$)/i
+
+  @doc """
+  Returns a denial for direct GitHub integration commands and `:allow` for
+  commands that remain inside the supported BOS boundary.
+  """
+  @spec evaluate(term()) :: :allow | {:deny, denial()}
+  def evaluate(command) when is_list(command), do: command |> Enum.join(" ") |> evaluate()
+
+  def evaluate(command) when is_binary(command) do
+    normalized = normalize(command)
+
+    cond do
+      Regex.match?(@gh_token, normalized) ->
+        {:deny,
+         %{
+           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
+           reason: "Direct GitHub CLI execution is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
+           suggested_operation: gh_replacement(normalized)
+         }}
+
+      Regex.match?(@github_api, normalized) ->
+        {:deny,
+         %{
+           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
+           reason: "Direct GitHub REST or GraphQL access is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
+           suggested_operation: api_replacement(normalized)
+         }}
+
+      true ->
+        :allow
+    end
+  end
+
+  def evaluate(_command), do: :allow
+
+  defp normalize(command) do
+    command
+    |> String.replace(~r/["'\\]/, "")
+    |> String.replace(~r/\s+/, " ")
+    |> String.trim()
+    |> String.downcase()
+  end
+
+  defp gh_replacement(command) do
+    cond do
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:create|edit)/, command) -> "bos_create_pull_request"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:merge|close)/, command) -> "bos_request_merge"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:checks|status)/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request_checks"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\b/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+issue\b/, command) -> "bos_get_issue"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+release\b/, command) -> "bos_create_release"
+      true -> "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp api_replacement(command) do
+    cond do
+      String.contains?(command, "/pulls") or String.contains?(command, "/graphql") ->
+        "bos_get_pull_request or the corresponding bos-mcp pull-request operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/issues") ->
+        "bos_get_issue or the corresponding bos-mcp issue operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/deployments") ->
+        "bos_create_deployment or the corresponding bos-mcp deployment operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/releases") ->
+        "bos_create_release"
+
+

})

    case await_response(port, @turn_start_id) do
      {:ok, %{"turn" => %{"id" => turn_id}}} -> {:ok, turn_id}
      other -> other
    end
  end

  defp await_turn_completion(port, on_message, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue) do
    receive_loop(
      port,
      on_message,
      Config.settings!().codex.turn_timeout_ms,
      "",
      tool_executor,
      auto_approve_requests,
      issue
    )
  end

  defp receive_loop(port, on_message, timeout_ms, pending_line, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue) do
    receive do
      {^port, {:data, {:eol, chunk}}} ->
        complete_line = pending_line <> to_string(chunk)
        handle_incoming(port, on_message, complete_line, timeout_ms, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue)

      {^port, {:data, {:noeol, chunk}}} ->
        receive_loop(
          port,
          on_message,
          timeout_ms,
          pending_line <> to_string(chunk),
          tool_executor,
          auto_approve_requests,
          issue
        )

      {^port, {:exit_status, status}} ->
        {:error, {:port_exit, status}}
    after
      timeout_ms ->
        {:error, :turn_timeout}
    end
  end

  defp handle_incoming(port, on_message, data, timeout_ms, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue) do
    payload_string = to_string(data)

    case Jason.decode(payload_string) do
      {:ok, %{"method" => "turn/completed"} = payload} ->
        emit_turn_event(on_message, :turn_completed, payload, payload_string, port, payload)
        {:ok, :turn_completed}

      {:ok, %{"method" => "turn/failed", "params" => _} = payload} ->
        emit_turn_event(
          on_message,
          :turn_failed,
          payload,
          payload_string,
          port,
          Map.get(payload, "params")
        )

        {:error, {:turn_failed, Map.get(payload, "params")}}

      {:ok, %{"method" => "turn/cancelled", "params" => _} = payload} ->
        emit_turn_event(
          on_message,
          :turn_

item.aggregatedOutput:
})

    case await_response(port, @turn_start_id) do
      {:ok, %{"turn" => %{"id" => turn_id}}} -> {:ok, turn_id}
      other -> other
    end
  end

  defp await_turn_completion(port, on_message, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue) do
    receive_loop(
      port,
      on_message,
      Config.settings!().codex.turn_timeout_ms,
      "",
      tool_executor,
      auto_approve_requests,
      issue
    )
  end

  defp receive_loop(port, on_message, timeout_ms, pending_line, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue) do
    receive do
      {^port, {:data, {:eol, chunk}}} ->
        complete_line = pending_line <> to_string(chunk)
        handle_incoming(port, on_message, complete_line, timeout_ms, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue)

      {^port, {:data, {:noeol, chunk}}} ->
        receive_loop(
          port,
          on_message,
          timeout_ms,
          pending_line <> to_string(chunk),
          tool_executor,
          auto_approve_requests,
          issue
        )

      {^port, {:exit_status, status}} ->
        {:error, {:port_exit, status}}
    after
      timeout_ms ->
        {:error, :turn_timeout}
    end
  end

  defp handle_incoming(port, on_message, data, timeout_ms, tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue) do
    payload_string = to_string(data)

    case Jason.decode(payload_string) do
      {:ok, %{"method" => "turn/completed"} = payload} ->
        emit_turn_event(on_message, :turn_completed, payload, payload_string, port, payload)
        {:ok, :turn_completed}

      {:ok, %{"method" => "turn/failed", "params" => _} = payload} ->
        emit_turn_event(
          on_message,
          :turn_failed,
          payload,
          payload_string,
          port,
          Map.get(payload, "params")
        )

        {:error, {:turn_failed, Map.get(payload, "params")}}

      {:ok, %{"method" => "turn/cancelled", "params" => _} = payload} ->
        emit_turn_event(
          on_message,
          :turn_cancelled,
          payload,
          payload_string,
          port,
          Map.get(payload, "params")
        )

        {:error, {:turn_cancelled, Map.get(payload, "params")}}

      {:ok, %{"method" => method} = payload}
      when is_binary(method) ->
        handle_turn_method(
          port,
          on_message,
          payload,
          payload_string,
          method,
          timeout_ms,
          tool_executor,
          auto_approve_requests,
          issue
        )

      {:ok, payload} ->
        emit_message(
          on_message,
          :other_message,
          %{
            payload: payload,
            raw: payload_string
          },
          metadata_from_message(port, payload)
        )

        receive_loop(port, on_message, timeout_ms, "", tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue)

      {:error, _reason} ->
        log_non_json_stream_line(payload_string, "turn stream")

        if protocol_message_candidate?(payload_string) do
          emit_message(
            on_message,
            :malformed,
            %{
              payload: payload_string,
              raw: payload_string
            },
            metadata_from_message(port, %{raw: payload_string})
          )
        end

        receive_loop(port, on_message, timeout_ms, "", tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue)
    end
  end

  defp emit_turn_event(on_message, event, payload, payload_string, port, payload_details) do
    emit_message(
      on_message,
      event,
      %{
        payload: payload,
        raw: payload_string,
        details: payload_details
      },
      metadata_from_message(port, payload)
    )
  end

  defp handle_turn_method(
         port,
         on_message,
         payload,
         payload_string,
         method,
         timeout_ms,
         tool_executor,
         auto_approve_requests,
         issue
       ) do
    metadata = metadata_from_message(port, payload)

    case maybe_handle_approval_request(
           port,
           method,
           payload,
           payload_string,
           on_message,
           metadata,
           tool_executor,
           auto_approve_requests,
           issue
         ) do
      :input_required ->
        emit_message(
          on_message,
          :turn_input_required,
          %{payload: payload, raw: payload_string},
          metadata
        )

        {:error, {:turn_input_required, payload}}

      :approved ->
        receive_loop(port, on_message, timeout_ms, "", tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue)

      :approval_required ->
        emit_message(
          on_message,
          :approval_required,
          %{payload: payload, raw: payload_string},
          metadata
        )

        {:error, {:approval_required, payload}}

      :unhandled ->
        if needs_input?(method, payload) do
          emit_message(
            on_message,
            :turn_input_required,
            %{payload: payload, raw: payload_string},
            metadata
          )

          {:error, {:turn_input_required, payload}}
        else
          emit_message(
            on_message,
            :notification,
            %{
              payload: payload,
              raw: payload_string
            },
            metadata
          )

          Logger.debug("Codex notification: #{inspect(method)}")
          receive_loop(port, on_message, timeout_ms, "", tool_executor, auto_approve_requests, issue)
        end
    end
  end

  defp maybe_handle_approval_request(
         port,
         "item/commandExecution/requestApproval",
         %{"id" => id} = payload,
         payload_string,
         on_message,
         metadata,
         _tool_executor,
         auto_approve_requests
       ) do
    approve_or_require(
      port,
      id,
      "acceptForSession",
      payload,
      payload_string,
      on_message,
      metadata,
      auto_approve_requests
    )
  end

diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..bb00efddd22b8ae7a56038c4d1b9e02893e379f8
--- /dev/null
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/command_policy.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+defmodule SymphonyElixir.Codex.CommandPolicy do
+  @moduledoc """
+  Enforces integration boundaries on commands requested by BOS AgentRuns.
+
+  This policy is deliberately evaluated on the complete shell command before
+  Codex receives permission to launch it. Host-side workspace hooks and other
+  Symphony administration do not pass through this module.
+  """
+
+  @type denial :: %{
+          reason: String.t(),
+          suggested_operation: String.t(),
+          policy: String.t()
+        }
+
+  @gh_token ~r/(?:^|\s)(?:[^\s;|&()]*\/)?gh(?:\s|$|[;|&()])/i
+  @github_api ~r/(?:https?:\/\/)?api\.github\.com(?:[\/:?#]|$)/i
+
+  @doc """
+  Returns a denial for direct GitHub integration commands and `:allow` for
+  commands that remain inside the supported BOS boundary.
+  """
+  @spec evaluate(term()) :: :allow | {:deny, denial()}
+  def evaluate(command) when is_list(command), do: command |> Enum.join(" ") |> evaluate()
+
+  def evaluate(command) when is_binary(command) do
+    normalized = normalize(command)
+
+    cond do
+      Regex.match?(@gh_token, normalized) ->
+        {:deny,
+         %{
+           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
+           reason: "Direct GitHub CLI execution is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
+           suggested_operation: gh_replacement(normalized)
+         }}
+
+      Regex.match?(@github_api, normalized) ->
+        {:deny,
+         %{
+           policy: "bos.integration.github.v1",
+           reason: "Direct GitHub REST or GraphQL access is prohibited for BOS AgentRuns.",
+           suggested_operation: api_replacement(normalized)
+         }}
+
+      true ->
+        :allow
+    end
+  end
+
+  def evaluate(_command), do: :allow
+
+  defp normalize(command) do
+    command
+    |> String.replace(~r/["'\\]/, "")
+    |> String.replace(~r/\s+/, " ")
+    |> String.trim()
+    |> String.downcase()
+  end
+
+  defp gh_replacement(command) do
+    cond do
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:create|edit)/, command) -> "bos_create_pull_request"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:merge|close)/, command) -> "bos_request_merge"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\s+(?:checks|status)/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request_checks"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+pr\b/, command) -> "bos_get_pull_request"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+issue\b/, command) -> "bos_get_issue"
+      Regex.match?(~r/(?:^|\s)gh\s+release\b/, command) -> "bos_create_release"
+      true -> "the corresponding bos-mcp domain operation"
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp api_replacement(command) do
+    cond do
+      String.contains?(command, "/pulls") or String.contains?(command, "/graphql") ->
+        "bos_get_pull_request or the corresponding bos-mcp pull-request operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/issues") ->
+        "bos_get_issue or the corresponding bos-mcp issue operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/deployments") ->
+        "bos_create_deployment or the corresponding bos-mcp deployment operation"
+
+      String.contains?(command, "/releases") ->
+        "bos_create_release"
+
+
